prompt: Write a Python function named sum_sequence that returns the sum of a list of integers.
Write a Python function named reverse_words that reverses the order of words in a sentence while preserving each word.
Write a Python function named count_frequencies that returns a dictionary mapping each item in a list to its frequency.
Write Python code to read a UTF-8 text file and return a list of non-empty stripped lines.
Write a Python function named load_json_file that loads a JSON file from disk and returns the parsed object.
Write a Python function that safely divides two numbers and returns None when division by zero occurs.
Write a pytest test for a function add(a, b) that should return the sum of two numbers.
Create a Python dataclass named User with fields id, name, and email.
Write a Python function that sorts a list of dictionaries by the key score in descending order.
Write a small Python argparse snippet that accepts --input and --output file paths.
Explain the time complexity of binary search and why it is logarithmic.
Write pseudocode for breadth-first search over a graph starting from a source node.
Describe when you would use a hash map instead of a list for lookup-heavy workloads.
A Python script is slow because it repeatedly appends rows to a pandas DataFrame in a loop. Explain a better approach.
A function has a mutable default argument bug. Explain the problem and show the safer pattern.
Explain what a memory leak is and name two ways to diagnose one in a long-running service.
Explain the difference between latency and throughput in an inference service.
Explain why batching can improve GPU throughput but may increase time-to-first-token.
Explain KV-cache reuse during autoregressive transformer decoding.
Explain the accept/reject idea behind speculative decoding at a high level.
Explain why a smaller draft model can speed up single-stream decoding when its predictions match the target model.
Write a SQL query that selects the top 10 users by total_spend from a users table.
Write a SQL query that counts orders per customer from an orders table.
Write a Python regex snippet that extracts email addresses from a string.
Write a FastAPI route named /healthz that returns a JSON object with status ok.
Show how to stream server-sent events from a Python web endpoint at a high level.
Explain why hardcoding API keys in source code is dangerous and what to do instead.
Explain one risk of using pickle on untrusted input and name a safer alternative for simple data.
Write a shell command to find all .py files under the current directory.
Explain what chmod +x does to a script file.
